key_learnings: Chronic low-grade inflammation is a root driver of most non-communicable diseases including CVD, type-2 diabetes, cancer, and autoimmune conditions. Omega-3 fatty acids, polyphenols, curcumin, and dietary fiber are the primary anti-inflammatory dietary compounds. High intake of refined carbohydrates, trans fats, and processed meats consistently elevates systemic inflammatory markers.
research_findings: The Dietary Inflammatory Index (DII) predicts C-reactive protein (CRP) levels and disease risk across large population studies. Curcumin (from turmeric) reduces CRP and IL-6 comparably to low-dose NSAIDs in patients with metabolic syndrome. A Mediterranean dietary pattern reduces CRP by ~20% compared to standard Western diet in 12-week intervention trials.
current_focus_areas: Building a personalized Dietary Inflammatory Index scoring tool; developing anti-inflammatory recipe and meal planning libraries; researching synergistic combinations of anti-inflammatory foods and spices.
